title: Cyclosorus mesocarpus
intro: Cyclosorus mesocarpus, also known as the mesocarp fern or Mesocarpus fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus mesocarpus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus mesocarpus is classified under the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. This species falls under the genus Cyclosorus, which is a type of fern that is native to tropical regions. The classification of Cyclosorus mesocarpus is further broken down into the order Polypodiales, class Polypodiopsida, and division Pteridophyta. This fern is also known as the tropical wood fern or the climbing wood fern. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the current taxonomy.
